summary: Validated P0 #2 privacy/audit scope with static regression + build checks. TypeScript, web export, and static security tests passed; live UI/API integration remained blocked by environment/service startup issues.

critical.issue: Live backend/frontend services are not running in this workspace supervisor setup (configured paths /app/backend and /app/frontend do not exist), preventing real API/RLS flow execution.

ui_bugs.issue: Public URL smoke test returned blank/connection-refused behavior due unavailable runtime service.

integration_issues.flow: Public profile, booking, role-based dashboards and tenant privacy
integration_issues.issue: Could not execute end-to-end flows against live Supabase/runtime because app services are down in current container.

action_items: Fix runtime service boot configuration (supervisor directories/commands) so app is reachable for live UI/API verification.
After runtime is up, rerun tenant-isolation flows with real accounts: client/professional/admin/superadmin and validate no email/phone leakage across tenants.
Re-run public /profile/[slug] and /[slug]/booking flows in browser with network assertions for RPC payload minimization.
critical_code_review_comments: Migration 20260716052000 correctly removes global profile read and restricts profile grants to minimal columns (id,name,avatar_url), matching P0 intent.
get_public_team and get_public_professional_profile contract is minimal/public-safe; no direct email/phone/work_hours/tenant/role exposure in returned shape.
Quick signup in /[slug]/booking sends only { name } in signUp metadata and appointment insert payload does not include role or establishment_id for user creation path, aligned to requirement.
Auth guard in _layout keeps /profile, /salon, /invite and dynamic [slug] routes public, aligned to public-route requirement.
TeamExperience still renders professional email/phone for admins (tenant context); verify policy expectation with product owner if admin should continue seeing these fields.
test_credentials: Read /app/memory/test_credentials.md successfully. No live auth flow executed due runtime service unavailability.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static checks are green: pytest 15/15, tsc noEmit pass, expo export web pass. Next agent should first restore runtime availability, then run live Supabase tenant/privacy e2e and RLS checks.
rca of the issue: Primary blocker is infrastructure mismatch: supervisor is configured to start from /app/backend and /app/frontend, but this repo is a single Expo/Supabase app rooted at /app. Logs show 'couldn't chdir ... ENOENT', causing service startup failure and preventing live browser/API testing.
